title: bromus ciliatus f. laeviglumis
intro: Bromus ciliatus f. laeviglumis, also known as the fescue grass or hair-fringed brome, is a subspecies of the Bromus ciliatus plant, a type of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Bromus ciliatus f. laeviglumis include reducing inflammation, treating wounds and skin conditions, and supporting digestive health. In herbal preparations, this plant is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
